text_a: (CNN) -- Callin' 2010 "The best year in safety performance in our company's history," Transocean Ltd., owner of the Gulf of Mexico oil rig that blew up, killin' 11 workers, done gave its top executives big bonuses and raises, 'cording to a recent filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. That includes a $200,000 salary bump for Transocean president and chief executive officer Steven L. Newman, whose base salary gon' jump from $900,000 to $1.1 million, 'cording to the SEC report. Newman's bonus was $374,062, the report say. Newman also got a $5.4 million long-term compensation package the company hooked him up with when he started as CEO in March 2010, 'cording to the SEC filing. The latest cash awards are based partly on the company's "performance under safety," the Transocean filing say. "Despite the tragic loss of life in the Gulf of Mexico, we pulled off an exemplary statistical safety record as measured by our total recordable incident rate and total potential severity rate," the SEC statement reads. "As measured by these standards, we recorded the best year in safety performance in our Company
text_b: List some effects from that 2010 Deepwater Horizon explosion. Hurt 17 workers
